title: andersonia lehmanniana subsp. pubescens
intro: Andersonia lehmanniana subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy Andersonia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to Tasmania, Australia, and is a low-growing, shrubby plant with white or pink flowers. The hairy Andersonia has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal purposes.
